attributes.title: Websites & Web Apps
attributes.shortDescription: Custom websites and web applications built for your business.
attributes.longDescription:
## Professional Web Development

We build modern, responsive websites and web applications that drive your business forward. From simple marketing sites to complex web applications with custom functionality.

### What We Build

- **Marketing Websites**: Fast, SEO-optimized sites that convert visitors into customers
- **E-commerce**: Online stores with secure checkout, inventory management, and shipping integration
- **Web Applications**: Custom dashboards, booking systems, client portals, and business tools
- **Content Management**: Easy-to-update websites built on modern CMS platforms

### Our Process

1. **Discovery**: We learn about your business, goals, and target audience
2. **Design**: Modern, mobile-first designs that reflect your brand
3. **Development**: Clean code, fast performance, accessibility best practices
4. **Launch**: Domain setup, hosting, SSL, and training
5. **Support**: Ongoing maintenance, updates, and technical support

### Technology Stack

- Next.js, React, TypeScript for modern web apps
- Tailwind CSS for responsive design
- Strapi or WordPress for content management
- Cloudflare for performance and security

### Included Services

- Domain registration and DNS management
- Professional business email setup
- SSL certificate and security hardening
- Google Analytics and SEO configuration
- Mobile-responsive design
- Contact forms and integrations

attributes.title: Wi-Fi & Network Upgrades
attributes.shortDescription: Enterprise-grade wireless networks with full coverage, VLANs for security, and seamless roaming. No more dead zones.
attributes.longDescription:
## Enterprise Wi-Fi for Small Businesses

Tired of slow Wi-Fi, dead zones, and devices that won't stay connected? We design and install enterprise-grade wireless networks that deliver fast, reliable coverage throughout your entire space.

### What You Get

- **Full Coverage**: Eliminate dead zones with professional access point placement
- **Fast Performance**: Wi-Fi 6/6E technology with gigabit speeds
- **Seamless Roaming**: Move throughout your space without dropping connection
- **Guest Networks**: Isolated guest Wi-Fi that keeps your business network secure
- **VLANs**: Network segmentation for security cameras, IoT devices, and departments
- **Centralized Management**: Monitor and manage your entire network from one dashboard

### Typical Installation Includes

- Site survey and RF planning
- 2-6 enterprise access points (Ubiquiti UniFi or TP-Link Omada)
- Managed PoE switch (powers access points over ethernet)
- Network firewall with VPN for remote access
- Professional cable runs and mounting
- Configuration, testing, and documentation
- 90 days of support and monitoring

### Perfect For

- Offices with 5-50 employees
- Multi-story buildings
- Warehouses and retail spaces
- Medical and dental offices
- Co-working spaces
- Restaurants and hospitality

### Why Enterprise Equipment?

Consumer routers can't handle the demands of a business environment. Enterprise access points are designed for:

- More concurrent users
- Better range and coverage
- Advanced security features
- Centralized management
- Long-term reliability

### Pricing

Most small business network upgrades range from $3,500 to $7,000 depending on space size and requirements.

attributes.title: IP Cameras & NVR
attributes.shortDescription: PoE security cameras with high-definition recording, remote viewing, motion alerts, and secure storage.
attributes.longDescription:
## Professional Security Camera Systems

Protect your business with high-definition IP cameras, secure recording, and remote viewing from anywhere.

### What's Included

- **PoE IP Cameras**: 4K resolution, night vision, weatherproof (indoor/outdoor)
- **NVR or NAS**: Network video recorder with 1-4TB storage (expandable)
- **Remote Access**: View live and recorded footage from your phone or computer
- **Motion Detection**: Smart alerts when motion is detected in specific zones
- **Secure Storage**: Encrypted local storage or optional cloud backup
- **Professional Installation**: Clean cable runs, secure mounting, configuration

### Camera Options

- **Dome Cameras**: Discreet, vandal-resistant, 360° coverage
- **Bullet Cameras**: Long-range, weatherproof, ideal for perimeters
- **Turret Cameras**: Wide-angle, indoor/outdoor, easy to aim
- **PTZ Cameras**: Pan, tilt, zoom with remote control

### Typical Systems

- **4-Camera System**: $2,500–$3,500 (small retail, office entry points)
- **8-Camera System**: $4,000–$6,000 (retail store, warehouse)
- **12+ Camera System**: $7,000+ (large retail, multi-building)

### Recording Options

- **Continuous**: Record 24/7 (storage capacity determines retention)
- **Motion-Triggered**: Record only when motion is detected (longer retention)
- **Scheduled**: Record during business hours only

### Why PoE Cameras?

Power over Ethernet (PoE) means each camera gets power and data through a single cable:

- Cleaner installation (no separate power adapters)
- More reliable (enterprise switches with battery backup)
- Easier troubleshooting
- Scalable (add cameras as you grow)

### Remote Viewing

Access your cameras from:

- Mobile app (iOS/Android)
- Web browser (any device)
- Desktop software (Windows/Mac)

All access is encrypted and protected with strong authentication.

attributes.title: Law Office Wi-Fi Upgrade
attributes.problem: Spotty Wi-Fi in conference rooms and dead zones throughout the office. Client meetings frequently interrupted by connectivity issues.
attributes.solution: Installed 4 Ubiquiti UniFi access points with seamless roaming and VLAN segmentation for guest Wi-Fi. Replaced aging network switch with managed PoE switch. Configured VPN for secure remote access.

attributes.title: Retail Security System
attributes.problem: Outdated analog camera system with poor image quality. No remote viewing capability. Tape-based recording system failing.
attributes.solution: Deployed 12 PoE IP cameras (mix of dome and bullet) with 4K recording to Synology NAS. Configured motion detection zones, mobile app access, and automated backups to cloud storage.

attributes.question: What types of businesses do you work with?
attributes.answer: We specialize in small businesses (5–50 employees) and home offices. This includes professional services (law, accounting, consulting), retail, healthcare, hospitality, and creative studios.

attributes.question: How much does a typical network upgrade cost?
attributes.answer: Most small business network upgrades range from $3,500 to $7,000 depending on the size of your space, number of users, and specific requirements (cameras, VLANs, VPN, etc.). We provide a detailed quote before any work begins.

attributes.question: Can you upgrade my existing network or do I need to start from scratch?
attributes.answer: In most cases, we can upgrade your existing infrastructure. We'll assess what you have, identify bottlenecks, and integrate new equipment seamlessly. Sometimes we recommend replacing aging equipment for reliability, but we'll always explain why and provide options.

attributes.question: What brands of equipment do you install?
attributes.answer: We primarily use Ubiquiti UniFi and TP-Link Omada for networking, Synology for storage/backups, and a variety of PoE IP cameras depending on your needs. We choose equipment based on reliability, performance, and long-term support.

attributes.question: How quickly can you respond to issues?
attributes.answer: For managed service clients, we guarantee a 4-hour response time during business hours (Mon–Fri 9am–6pm CT). Non-managed clients are supported on a best-effort basis, typically within 1–2 business days.

attributes.tagline: Digital infrastructure for homes and small businesses
